uplink.description: GNSS position, live on-device fix (lat/lon float32 BE) with fix time
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds fix; bytes built so the big-endian IEEE-754 float32 lon=121.5, lat=31.25 and the u32 fix epoch=1700000000. Two deliberate forks from reference/upstream-codec.js: (1) upstream's hex2float divides the mantissa by 0x7fffff instead of 0x800000, an off-by-one that makes it decode these bytes as 121.50000685/31.25000182; we use the correct IEEE-754 divisor (0x800000) and recover the exact encoded 121.5/31.25. (2) upstream emits the timestamp shifted +8h (CST applied to an already-UTC epoch); we emit the true UTC instant 2023-11-14T22:13:20Z.

uplink.description: Heartbeat: battery volts, link quality, temperature, vibration -> motion
uplink._note: Synthetic heartbeat; expected.data computed from reference/upstream-codec.js (vol byte 220 -> 1.5+220*0.01 = 3.7 V mapped to battery; rssi byte 95 -> -95 dBm; SNR-included flag set, snr 150*0.01 = 1.5 dB; gpsstate nibble 3 -> located; vibstate 3 -> vibrationLevel 3 and action.motion.detected; temperature 23; movement 12*5 = 60 s).

uplink.description: Position beacon: BLE scan (cloud-solved) surfaced as bleBeacons extra
uplink._note: Synthetic position-beacon (type 0x7, length nibble 2); two 5-byte beacon records at offset 6. major/minor are upstream's uppercase hex pairs; rssi byte - 256. BLE scans are positioning inputs requiring a cloud solver, so they are extras, never position.*.

uplink.description: Asset beacon: BLE asset scan surfaced as bleBeacons extra
uplink._note: Synthetic asset-beacon (type 0x8, qty 1); one 5-byte record at offset 2. Matches reference/upstream-codec.js AssetBeacon_Proc field extraction, remapped to the bleBeacons extra.
Synthetic alarm (type 0x9). Upstream's Alarm_proc never reads the payload flag (a bug: it tests an uninitialised local); we read the alarm bit from byte 1 so a set flag is reported as magnetRemoved.
Synthetic shock-detection (type 0xA); count is the big-endian u16 at bytes 1-2, matching reference/upstream-codec.js ShockDetection_proc.

uplink.description: Unsupported message type yields an error
uplink._note: Synthetic error vector: high-nibble message type 0xF is not defined by the protocol (upstream returns null for this; we report an error).
